title: keteleeria davidiana subsp. formosana
intro: Keteleeria davidiana subsp. formosana, also known as the Taiwanese or Formosan golden fir, is a subspecies of coniferous tree native to Taiwan. The medicinal properties of this plant involve its use in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. Studies have shown that Keteleeria davidiana subsp. formosana has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
